Job Description:

Collaborate with Innovative 3Mers Around the World

Choosing where to start and grow your career has a major impact on your professional and personal life, so it’s equally important you know that the company that you choose to work at, and its leaders, will support and guide you. With a wide variety of people, global locations, technologies and products, 3M is a place where you can collaborate with other curious, creative 3Mers.

The Impact You’ll Make in this Role  

Sr. Cloud Platform Engineer role is part of the IT Platform Engineering team which enables Cloud & DevOps practice across 3M. This role focuses on implementation, refactor, re-platform, and rearchitect of applications and application E2E deployment processes for 3M’s Public Cloud (Azure/AWS). We also support building automation to manage our source code management system (GitHub), deploying cloud resources (Azure/AWS), and Operations & support IT Infra for global 3M teams.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Designs and develop next generation cloud platforms for 3M to support large scale server/application workloads. 

  • Advocates for engineering practices and assists teams in using frameworks for infrastructure as code and immutable infrastructure concepts.

  • Write, maintain, and support automation scripts that integrate with GitHub, Azure Active Directory, Microsoft Teams, and Azure subscriptions.

  • Troubleshoot, patch, and maintain existing automation scripts, infrastructure, and deployed cloud resources.

  • Deploy and manage Azure Functions, Logic Apps, Service Bus queues/topics, Azure Storage (Blob/Queue), Security, Networking, Azure Local and other Azure cloud resources.

  • Builds full and partial stack components for hybrid, Azure, and on-premises solutions 

  • Develops Azure cloud native templates

  • Ensures the quality, consistency, and performance of the cloud platform through fully automated deployments, functional and performance testing.

  • Works as a member of global infrastructure engineering responsible to design, develop and operate cloud platforms which runs 24x7.

Required Experience and Skills

  • Bachelor’s degree in information systems, Computer Science, or a related field — or equivalent experience from an accredited university.

  • Minimum 6-9 years of hands-on experience in IT (4 yrs with DevOps).

  • Experience in with Frontend UI development (node.js or similar) and hands-on with Python & Yaml/Typescript.

Optional Experience and Skills

  • Demonstrated knowledge of IaaS, PaaS, and virtualization – service-oriented architecture designed around the delivery of Infrastructure components as a service 

  • Proficiency in at least three of the following: ARM, CFT, CDK, Python, PowerShell, Terraform, JSON, Node.js, Java, JavaScript 

  • Working knowledge of traditional IT systems to include Operating Systems, Platforms, Database, Automation, Storage, and Data Protection 

  • Working knowledge or experience delivering modern applications, microservices, and services within a CICD / DevOps model 

  • Familiarity with industry compliance and security standards including China MLPS, PCI DSS, SOC-1&2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, ITAR, CJIS and FDA.

Work Location & Additional Information

Bangalore, Karnataka (Hybrid)

Learn more about 3M’s creative solutions to the world’s problems at www.3M.com or on Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn @3M.

Safety is a core value at 3M. All employees are expected to contribute to a strong Environmental Health and Safety (EHS) culture by following safety policies, identifying hazards, and engaging in continuous improvement.

Job Title: Sr Specialist - Sr Specialist – Credit Risk

Collaborate with Innovative 3Mers Around the World

Choosing where to start and grow your career has a major impact on your professional and personal life, so it’s equally important you know that the company that you choose to work at, and its leaders, will support and guide you. With a wide variety of people, global locations, technologies and products, 3M is a place where you can collaborate with other curious, creative 3Mers.

The Impact You’ll Make in this Role

  • Supporting global credit risk teams through comprehensive credit data analysis and assisting in the creation of effective portfolio problem‑solving models.

  • Participating in cross‑functional teams to identify opportunities and support initiatives that drive process improvements, centralization, and standardization.

  • Implementing robust data integrity and quality assurance checks to ensure the reliability, consistency, and standardization of reporting outputs.

Your Skills and Expertise 

To set you up for success in this role from day one, 3M requires (at a minimum) the following qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree or higher (completed and verified prior to start) in data analytics, statistics, economics, finance, or a related field
  • Six(6) or more years of experience in data analytics, reporting, or similar roles working with large and complex datasets in an organization
  • Experience in Credit, Collections or Order to Cash
  • Experience using SAP

Additional qualifications that could help you succeed even further in this role include:

  • Master’s degree in data analytics, statistics, economics, finance, or a related discipline from an accredited institution
  • Experience analyzing large datasets using mathematical, statistical, and quantitative techniques, including data mining and complex analysis
  • Expertise in Power BI or similar visualization platforms, and experience with ETL/automation tools

Work Location: Hebbal, Bangalore (Karnataka)

Mode of Working: Hybrid (2-3 days in a week)

Supporting Your Well-being

3M offers many programs to help you live your best life – both physically and financially. To ensure competitive pay and benefits, 3M regularly benchmarks with other companies that are comparable in size and scope.

 Sr. Data Engineer-HANA, Snowflake, SAP BW, ABAP 

Collaborate with Innovative 3Mers Around the World

Choosing where to start and grow your career has a major impact on your professional and personal life, so it’s equally important you know that the company that you choose to work at, and its leaders, will support and guide you. With a wide variety of people, global locations, technologies and products, 3M is a place where you can collaborate with other curious, creative 3Mers.

The Impact You’ll Make in this Role

As a Sr. Data Engineer, you will have the opportunity to design, develop, and deliver high-performance enterprise analytics and data warehousing solutions leveraging SAP HANA (advanced SQL/SQL Script), Snowflake, SAP BW on HANA, ABAP. This role requires a deep understanding of SAP HANA architecture along with SQL expertise, Snowflake and SAP BW on HANA data modeling techniques, and practical experience in ETL/ELT processes involving Snowflake, ensuring seamless data flow and high-performance analytics capabilities.

As a Sr. Data Engineer, you will have the opportunity to tap into your curiosity and collaborate with some of the most innovative people around the world. Here, you will make an impact by:

  • Building HANA models using Calculation Views (Graphical & Scripted), Stored procedures, optimized for real-time reporting and data consumption.
  • Expertise in data modeling, SQL scripting, stored procedures, and performance tuning.
  • Leveraging Snowflake features with your expertise such as scripting, time travel, virtual warehouses, and query optimization.
  • Experience with Snowflake Advanced features including Snow pipe, Streams, Tasks, and Stored Procedures.
  • Expertise in advanced SQL like writing and optimizing complex SQL queries to handle large data volumes efficiently.
  • Collaborating with data architects and business stakeholders to design scalable and efficient data models in both SAP HANA and Snowflake, supporting current and future analytical needs.
  • Deep understanding of data generation, transformation, and consumption across the full SAP Order-to-Cash lifecycle, including key tables, document flows, and SD–FI dependencies. Ability to trace complete data lineage from source transactions to downstream analytics and reporting layers.
  • Hands-on experience extracting and integrating OTC data from SAP ECC/S/4HANA into enterprise data platforms.
  • Strong ability to convert SAP OTC business rules (pricing, billing, revenue recognition, returns) into trusted analytical datasets.
  • Acting as a bridge between SAP functional teams and analytics users to deliver business-aligned insights and KPIs.
  • Designing and developing SAP BW on HANA solutions including ADSOs, Composite Providers, Open ODS Views, and multi-layered data models.
  • Design and optimize ETL processes using BW transformations, DTPs, Process Chains, and HANA-based data flows.
  • Proactively monitor and optimize query performance, data loads, and overall system health for both the SAP HANA and Snowflake environments.
  • Developing and enhancing ABAP objects including Function Modules, Enhancements to support BW and HANA integrations.
  • Provide expert-level support for data issues, performance bottlenecks, and system failures, ensuring minimal downtime.

Your Skills and Expertise 
 

To set you up for success in this role from day one, 3M requires (at a minimum) the following qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree or higher (completed and verified prior to start)
  • 8 years of hands-on experience in HANA, Snowflake and advanced SQL/SQL Script, Additionally with SAP BW experience.
  • Deep understanding of data generation, transformation, and consumption across the full SAP Order-to-Cash lifecycle, including key tables, document flows, and SD–FI dependencies.
  • Expertise with Snowflake advanced features.
  • Previous experience in Developing and enhancing ABAP objects including Function Modules, Enhancements to support BW and HANA integrations.
  • Expertise in Native HANA models using Calculation Views (Graphical & Scripted).

Additional qualifications that could help you succeed even further in this role include:

  • Excellent communication with business stakeholders to gather, clarify, and validate requirements, ensuring a shared understanding across technical and non-technical teams.
  • Translating complex business needs into scalable HANA and Snowflake technical architecture designs.
  • To be acting as a bridge between business and technical teams, aligning data models, reporting needs, and performance expectations.
  • Producing clear documentation and presentations that explain architectural decisions, data flows, and system capabilities.

Location

  • 3M Bangalore Global Technical Center, Bengaluru, (Hybrid)

Learn more about 3M’s creative solutions to the world’s problems at www.3M.com or on Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn @3M.

Safety is a core value at 3M. All employees are expected to contribute to a strong Environmental Health and Safety (EHS) culture by following safety policies, identifying hazards, and engaging in continuous improvement.

Collaborate with Innovative 3Mers Around the World

Choosing where to start and grow your career has a major impact on your professional and personal life, so it’s equally important you know that the company that you choose to work at, and its leaders, will support and guide you. With a wide variety of people, global locations, technologies and products, 3M is a place where you can collaborate with other curious, creative 3Mers.

Position Name: Lead Analyst - HR Reporting Technology

We are seeking an experienced HR Reporting Technology Analyst with advanced expertise in HR processes, Power BI, and Workday reports.  In this role, you will be tasked with translating report requirements into deliverables that inform HR processes and drive data-informed actions.  Your experience in HR processes, particularly in compensation, and the creation of reporting solutions related to those processes will make you an integral part of our HR Technology and Analytics team.

Key Responsibilities

Data Reporting & Delivery (Core)

  • Develop and Maintain Reporting Solutions: Design, build, test, and deploy operational and strategic HR reports, dashboards, and scorecards using Workday Report Writer, Power BI and other system-specific reporting tools (i.e. Varicent for SIP Planning).
  • Data Visualization: Translate complex HR data (e.g., headcount, turnover, compensation, recruiting metrics) into clear, compelling, and actionable visualizations and insights using Power BI.
  • Ad-Hoc Reporting: Fulfill time-sensitive ad-hoc report requests utilizing HR data sources and our data warehouse.
  • Workday Report Management: Manage the lifecycle of Workday reports, including creating custom reports, maintaining calculated fields, and ensuring proper security configuration.

Technical Expertise & Data Integrity

  • System Integration: Understand the Workday HCM and other HR system data models to effectively source and structure data for reporting.  Integrating data from multiple data sources will be required.
  • Data Governance and Quality: Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and integrity across all reporting platforms and source systems.
  • Technical Documentation: Maintain comprehensive documentation for reporting solutions, including data definitions, report logic, data lineage, security configurations, and user guides.
  • Query and Scripting Proficiency: Utilize SQL and other relevant querying languages to extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from various sources into the reporting environment, as needed.

Stakeholder Collaboration & Consultation

  • Requirements Gathering: Consult with HR Business Partners, HR Leadership, Finance, and other business stakeholders to understand their analytical needs and translate them into technical report specifications.
  • Training and Support: Provide training and ongoing support to end-users on how to effectively access, navigate, and interpret reports and dashboards in Workday and Power BI.
  • Process Improvement: Proactively identify opportunities to improve reporting efficiency, automate manual data processes, and enhance the overall user experience.

Compliance & Security

  • Data Confidentiality: Ensure strict adherence to global and local data privacy regulations (e.g., GDPR, CCPA) and company policies when handling sensitive employee data.
  • Access Control: Manage and audit user access to reports and data within Power BI and Workday, ensuring compliance with defined security matrices and need-to-know principles.

Required Experience and Skills

  • Bachelor’s degree in information systems, Computer Science, or a related field — or equivalent experience.
  • Minimum of 8+ years of experience, including 5+ years in data analysis, data transformation, and report development using contemporary BI tools.
  • Minimum of 5 years of work experience in a global corporation within a Human Resources or Finance function, with strong knowledge of HR compensation practices.
  • Strong experience with advanced Power BI development and advanced SQL skills.
  • Strong knowledge of Workday or any Human Capital Management (HCM) systems.
  • Strong expertise in advanced Workday report development, including Prism Analytics and Discovery Boards.
  • Experience in integrating data from various sources into reporting solutions.

Additional skills which would help you to succeed in this role have

  • Strong communication skills to effectively engage with end users, ensuring clarity and alignment.
  • Experience in interpreting requirements and designing reports while maintaining a high level of detail.
  • Knowledge and experience with data warehousing solutions such as Snowflake or similar platforms.

Job Description:

Job Summary

This role is responsible for managing marketing activities for a portfolio of surgical and medical products in India. These products are used by hospitals and healthcare professionals to improve patient safety and surgical outcomes.

The role involves understanding customer needs, planning marketing activities, and supporting business growth by working closely with sales and technical teams.

Key Responsibilities

  • Good communication skills (written and spoken English)
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Working knowledge of MS Excel and PowerPoint

Work Style

  • Able to work independently with minimal supervision
  • Comfortable working with multiple teams
  • Willing to travel and interact with customers (hospitals, clinicians)

Why This Role Matters

This role contributes directly to improving surgical safety and patient outcomes by ensuring the right products and solutions reach healthcare professionals at the right time.

Job Description:

Collaborate with Innovative 3Mers Around the World  

Choosing where to start and grow your career has a major impact on your professional and personal life, so it’s equally important you know that the company that you choose to work at, and its leaders, will support and guide you. Job Description:

Job Description Summary

3M Corporate Audit department provides independent, objective assurance and consulting activities designed to add value and improve 3M's operations. It helps 3M accomplish its objectives by bringing a systematic, disciplined approach to evaluate and improve the effectiveness of risk management, control, and governance processes.

You will be part of the Asia Pacific Audit team to assist the corporation by performing financial and operational audits to ensure the accuracy of financial statements, adequacy of operational and internal controls, and identification of administrative efficiencies that will lead to savings in money and time. You will assist the corporation in complying with federal, state, and local laws and regulations through assigned audits. Also, you will interact with functions across the company and regions and be exposed to both financial and business operations.

Job Description

Responsibilities:

  • Lead and/ or work with the audit team in a fast learning environment to conduct financial, operational, and/ or compliance audits.
  • Assist audit management in risk assessment and audit planning.
  • Review and evaluate the adequacy and effectiveness of internal controls, compliance with Corporate and local regulations, policies and procedures.
  • Identify and clearly define audit issues and root causes, provide sound and practical improvements to internal controls and business processes.
  • Prepare audit reports/ audit findings and present them to the business management team.
  • Ensure that management action plans are developed, sustainable and implemented on time.
  • Prepare work papers and adequately document audit work performed to support conclusions reached.
  • Assess the entity control environment and provide risk & control advice to regional/ local management.
  • Actively participate in global/ regional initiatives aimed at improving Corporate Audit processes and procedures.

Requirements:

  • Degree in Accountancy, Business, or equivalent.
  • Professional certification (CIA, CPA, ACCA) is preferred.
  • At least 10 years of relevant experience in audit/ internal controls, preferably with corporate and/or Big 4 accounting firm exposure.
  • A highly motivated individual with demonstrated leadership attributes and problem-solving skills.
  • Has strong communication and team collaboration skills.
  • Possesses the drive to gain good knowledge and understanding of the Company's policies and procedures, process risks and controls.
  • This position may require some amount of travel, with the principle of safety first.
  • Experience in SAP ERP will be an added advantage.

Job Description:

Job Title: Sr Specialist - US State Income Tax

Collaborate with Innovative 3Mers Around the World

Choosing where to start and grow your career has a major impact on your professional and personal life, so it’s equally important you know that the company that you choose to work at, and its leaders, will support and guide you. With a wide variety of people, global locations, technologies and products, 3M is a place where you can collaborate with other curious, creative 3Mers.

The Impact You’ll Make in this Role
 

As a Sr Specialist - US State Income Tax , you will have the opportunity to tap into your curiosity and collaborate with some of the most innovative people around the world. Here, you will make an impact by:

  • Preparing U.S. state and local income tax returns, including separate, combined, and consolidated filings.
  • Assisting with state tax provisions, including preparing calculations, journal entries, and account reconciliations & supporting state income tax audits by preparing responses, gathering documentation, and analyzing proposed adjustments.
  • Performing state income tax calculations and maintain supporting workpapers & researching state tax technical issues and provide clear summaries and recommendations
  • Processing estimated tax payments, extensions, and state notices & partnering with the Global Tax and Finance teams to support the U.S. federal tax provisions and U.S. Federal income tax return as needed.
  • Participating in process improvement, automation, and standardization initiatives.

Your Skills and Expertise 
 

To set you up for success in this role from day one, 3M requires (at a minimum) the following qualifications:

  • Bachelor's degree or higher in Accounting, Finance or a related field
  • Professional certification such as CPA, CA, CMA, CIA, or CISA
  • At least 5 years of work experience in tax
  • Proficient in MS Excel and significant knowledge of SAP
  • Advanced proficiency in English & good oral/written communication skills.

Additional qualifications that could help you succeed even further in this role include:

  • Knowledge of tax accounting under U.S. GAAP
  • Experience with U.S. federal and state income tax returns
  • Experience with Corp tax and OneSource Income Tax Provision

Work Location: Hebbal, Bangalore (Karnataka)

Mode of Working: Hybrid (2-3 days in a week)

Supporting Your Well-being

3M offers many programs to help you live your best life – both physically and financially. To ensure competitive pay and benefits, 3M regularly benchmarks with other companies that are comparable in size and scope.

We provide the freedom to explore and encourage curiosity and creativity. We gain new insight from diverse thinking, and take risks on new ideas. Here, you can apply your talent in bold ways that matter.

Job Description:

We are seeking a highly experienced and strategic Insights & Analytics Manager to join our team at the 3M office in India, located ether at Bangalore or Mumbai. The Insights & Retail Analytics Manager will be part of the APAC Marketing Centre (AMC) team and play a critical role in driving insight led decision making for 3M’s Consumer Business in India and support select regional initiatives. This role partners primarily with India business teams and Asia Pacific regional partners to address key strategic business questions through consumer research, advanced analytics, and market intelligence. 
This is a critical dual-responsibility role that bridges the gap between market research (Insights) and data analytics (POS & Syndicated Data). The role is responsible for embedding consumer, shopper, and retail insights into innovation development, product positioning, packaging, communication strategy, media planning, and category growth initiatives. 
Reporting directly to the Asia Retail Analytics Lead, with a dotted line reporting structure to the Asia Insights Lead, this position requires a seasoned professional who can seamlessly navigate both qualitative/ quantitative consumer insights and quantitative retail data. You will collaborate closely with the IN business and cross functional teams-including portfolio management, marketing, sales, digital, media, and product development-will be essential to support growth.

A. Retail Analytics

Oversee POS and retail analytics, including internal sales data (sell-in, POS), Syn data: retail audit, household panel, and other ad-hoc analytics workstreams like assortment optimization, planogram testing, and modern trade channel / customer performance assessments.

  • [IN] Own POS & Syn data reporting for IN: by priority portfolio, by priority channel
  • [IN] Monthly reviews (POS and Syn data) with business teams to identify opportunities/issues
  • [IN] Own POS and Syn Data reporting on PBI: Own syndicated data analytics
  • [IN] Own annual pres reviews: NIQ and KWP: strategic annual pres as per issue/themes to diagnose/deep dive
  • [IN] Own any ad-hoc analytics (like distribution analytics)
  • [IN] Provide support and own ad-hoc analytics – MMM, Pricing, POG analytics
  • [Area specific] Area Syn data responsibility:
  • with country team: -annual negotiation / renewal support,
  • imparting training for leveraging syn data usage for decision making
  • reporting at area & global level

Some of the regular retail analytics activities will be:

  • Strategic BI Reporting:
    • Work with IN data delivery POC to develop and maintain automated PBI dashboards and comprehensive reports that provide the commercial team with timely and accurate visibility into market performance.
    • Point-of-Sale (POS) Analytics: Analyze retailer POS data to monitor sales trends, identify distribution gaps, and optimize assortment and pricing strategies.
    • Business Reviews: Lead regular business reviews with the IN team, utilizing syndicated and POS data to evaluate category performance, track market share, and assess competitive dynamics.

  • Syndicated Data Management:
    • Serve as the primary owner and subject matter expert for all syndicated data sources, (e.g., NielsenIQ Retail Audit RMS, Kantar Worldpanel KWP), you will extract, analyze, and interpret this data to uncover actionable business insights.
    • Agency Relationship Management: Manage relationships with external data providers (e.g., NielsenIQ Retail Audit RMS, Kantar Worldpanel KWP), ensuring data quality, negotiating contracts, and maximizing the value of these partnerships.

B. Consumer & Shopper Insights

The Consumer & Shopper Insights portion of this role focuses on understanding the consumer and shopper with respect to the brand's position in the Indian market.

  • Serve as the “voice of the consumer” for assigned categories and brands, ensuring insights are consistently integrated into India innovation, marketing, and business decisions.
  • Partner with business and AMC teams to identify growth opportunities, define learning agendas, and scope research questions that deliver actionable insights.
  • Design, manage, and execute Learning Plans -including qualitative, quantitative, social listening, to inform innovation, communication, and retail strategies.
  • Provide consultative guidance to India and APAC regional stakeholders on research methodologies and insight interpretation.
  • Elevate Insights function through new tools and techniques including digital ethnography, ecommerce analytics, and rapid cycle research.
  • Explore and embed AI-led tools for insight mining, testing and evaluation
  • Lead India Home Care brand health, communication tracking and support selected regional initiatives.
  • Initiate and innovation projects through needs exploration, idea screening, concept testing, product fit evaluation, and volumetric studies.
  • Develop and maintain social listening dashboards to monitor trends in key target group and mine consumer needs and pain point insights across Asia.

Some of the regular insights activities: (partnering with external insights agencies)

  • Brand Health Tracking: Design, execute, and analyze ongoing brand health tracking studies to monitor brand awareness, consideration, and equity over time. You will identify key drivers of brand performance and provide actionable recommendations to the marketing team.
  • Innovation Research: Lead consumer research to support the new product development pipeline. This includes concept testing, product testing, and identifying unmet consumer needs to guide innovation strategies.
  • Communication Research: Support creative development process in big idea generation and evaluation. Evaluate the effectiveness of marketing campaigns and advertising efforts through rigorous post-communication surveys. You will measure campaign reach, resonance, and impact on brand metrics.
  • Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner closely with the IN business and marketing teams to ensure that consumer insights are integrated into strategic planning and execution. You will act as the voice of the consumer in cross-functional meetings.
  • Regional Alignment: Maintain a strong dotted-line relationship with the Asia Insights Lead to ensure that local research initiatives align with regional methodologies and strategic priorities.

Qualifications and Skills

Required Experience/Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree or higher in Business Administration, Economics, Statistics, Engineering, or a related field from an accredited institution
  • At least 8-10 years of dedicated experience in Analytics / Syndicated Data management, with deep expertise in utilizing Nielsen retail audit and Kantar Worldpanel data.
  • 10+ years of consumer insights or market research experience in India (client‑side)
  • Strong command of qualitative, quantitative methodologies
  • Proven experience working within the Fast-Moving Consumer Goods (FMCG) or Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) industry is highly preferred.

Required Expertise/skills

  • Proven ability to analyze, integrate, and synthesize insights from multiple sources to influence business stakeholders
  • Demonstrated project management skills with experience managing multiple simultaneous projects
  • Resourceful, detail‑oriented, and able to work independently as well as collaboratively
  • Running Insights workshops for stakeholders
  • Analytical Acumen: Exceptional ability to synthesize complex data from multiple sources (primary research, syndicated data, POS) into clear, cohesive, and actionable business narratives.
  • Technical Proficiency: Advanced proficiency in data analysis and visualization tools. Experience with Power BI platforms is required. Working knowledge of SQL, Python, or SPSS is considered a strong advantage.
  • Communication and Presentation: Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present complex data findings to non-technical stakeholders in a compelling and easily understandable manner.
  • Stakeholder Management: Proven ability to build strong relationships and influence decision-making across matrixed organizations, working effectively with local marketing teams and regional leadership.
  • Vendor Management: Experience managing external research agencies and data providers, including budget management and contract negotiation.

Learn more about 3M’s creative solutions to the world’s problems at www.3M.com or on Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn @3M.

Job Description:

HR Technology Workday Lead – US Payroll, Time, & Absence

Collaborate with Innovative 3Mers Around the World
Choosing where to start and grow your career has a major impact on your professional and personal life, so it’s equally important you know that the company you choose will support and guide you. With a wide variety of people, global locations, technologies, and products, 3M is a place where you can collaborate with other curious, creative 3Mers.

The Impact You’ll Make in this Role

As an HR Technology Workday Lead for US Payroll, Time & Absence, you will have the opportunity to tap into your curiosity and collaborate with some of the most innovative people around the world. Here, you will make an impact by:

  • Acting as the expert in Workday Payroll, Time Tracking, and Absence, specializing in advanced configuration of U.S. pay rules, accruals, eligibility, earnings/deductions, and time valuation.
  • Driving global deployment, optimization, and governance of US Payroll, Time, and Absence solutions, ensuring consistent design, compliance, best practices and documentation.

Your Skills and Expertise

Basic Qualifications

To set you up for success in this role from day one, 3M requires (at a minimum):

  • Bachelor’s degree or higher (completed and verified prior to start)
  • Minimum eight (8) years of HR Technology experience.
  • Minimum eight (8) years of Workday configuration experience specializing in Payroll, Absence, and/or Time Tracking

Preferred Qualifications

Additional qualifications that could help you succeed even further in this role include:

  • Workday Pro certification preferred in US Payroll, Time, and/or Absence.
  • Strong expertise with US Workday Payroll.
  • Proven success designing complex pay calculation rules, accrual configurations, time valuation, eligibility, and payroll workflows.
  • Familiarity with union rules, shift differentials, premium pay, and complex pay components.
  • Strong analytical and troubleshooting abilities, particularly with pay results and time valuation.
  • Experience leading or mentoring analysts or technical team members.
  • Exceptional communication, analytical, and problem-solving skills.
  • Experience with Workday Scheduling module preferred.

Work Location & Additional Information

Bangalore, Karnataka — Hybrid Mode (2-3 days in a week)

Job Description:

About the Role

As an Area Sales Manager – Personal Safety Division, you will drive revenue growth for 3M’s Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) portfolio across Chennai and select regions of Tamil Nadu. The role focuses on expanding industrial market penetration, strengthening distributor networks, and building strong relationships with key industrial customers.

Product Portfolio

The role will promote and sell 3M Personal Protection Equipment (PPE) including:

  • Respiratory protection

  • Hearing protection

  • Eye and face protection

  • Welding safety solutions

  • Work-at-height safety equipment

  • Confined space safety solutions

Key Responsibilities

Sales & Business Growth

  • Achieve and exceed territory sales revenue targets across industrial customers.

  • Drive market penetration and share gain in assigned accounts and industries.

  • Develop and execute territory business plans and account strategies.

Customer Engagement

  • Manage end-user sales activities, including:

    • Customer visits

    • Product demonstrations

    • Safety training sessions

    • Industrial surveys

    • Maintenance and safety awareness programs

Market Development

  • Execute territory-specific marketing initiatives.

  • Conduct industrial exhibitions, awareness campaigns, and safety workshops.

  • Drive multi-contact programs and key account initiatives.

Distributor & Channel Management

  • Lead and motivate distributor sales representatives to achieve targets.

  • Collaborate with distributors to execute monthly sales plans.

  • Strengthen channel partner engagement and drive performance.

Market Intelligence

  • Provide insights on:

    • Competitive activities

    • Emerging industry trends

    • Customer needs and buying behavior

  • Deliver business presentations on 3M safety solutions to customers and management.

Target Customer Segments

  • Heavy Manufacturing & Industrial Plants

  • Pharmaceutical Manufacturing

  • Wind Blade Manufacturing

  • Chemical Industry

  • Auto Ancillary

  • Defense Manufacturing

  • General Industry

  • Oil & Gas

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Engineering or any discipline

  • MBA in Sales / Marketing (preferred)

Experience Required

  • 5+ years of experience in industrial B2B sales

  • Experience selling Capex and recurring consumable products to industrial customers.

Learn more about 3M’s creative solutions to the world’s problems at www.3M.com or on Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn @3M.

Job Description:

Area Sales Manager – Industrial Adhesives & Tapes (Territory Role) | Mumbai or Pune

Role Summary

The Area Sales Manager is responsible for driving profitable growth within the assigned territory by acquiring new customers, expanding share of wallet in existing accounts, and delivering differentiated application-based solutions. The role requires strong consultative selling capability, technical engagement, and disciplined execution to achieve Operating Plan (OP), OLCG, margin, and working capital objectives.

Key Responsibilities

1. Sales & Profitable Growth

  • Deliver monthly, quarterly, and annual revenue targets aligned to OP.

  • Drive New Business Development (NBD) through structured prospecting and opportunity management.

  • Execute account plans and opportunity strategies to expand product mix and margin.

  • Focus on value-based selling and protect pricing within defined guardrails.

2. Customer Engagement & Application Selling

  • Conduct regular customer visits, process audits, technical demonstrations, and product trials.

  • Identify process gaps and recommend adhesives/tapes solutions that enhance efficiency, quality, and total cost of ownership (TCO).

  • Collaborate with Technical Service/Application Engineers for successful trial validation and commercialization.

  • Build strong relationships with OEMs, converters, fabricators, and key accounts.

3. Territory & Funnel Management

  • Plan and manage territory coverage, call cadence, and visit productivity.

  • Maintain a healthy opportunity funnel (≥3× coverage) with accurate stage definition and probability.

  • Utilize CRM (e.g., Salesforce) for lead tracking, forecasting, and action planning.

  • Drive disciplined pipeline reviews to improve forecast accuracy.

4. Commercial & Operational Excellence

  • Prepare proposals and negotiate commercial terms within pricing guardrails.

  • Ensure timely invoicing, collections, DSO control, and overdue reduction.

  • Monitor competitor activities and market dynamics; implement counter-strategies.

  • Support aged inventory liquidation and stock allocation planning.

5. Cross-Functional Collaboration

  • Partner with Marketing for Ad Merch programs, campaigns, and demand generation initiatives.

  • Collaborate with Supply Chain for allocation management and backorder mitigation.

  • Align with Finance on credit management, CIR impact, and working capital control.

6. Compliance & Governance

  • Adhere to company policies on Ethics, EHS, Trade Compliance, and documentation.

  • Maintain accurate records of trials, approvals, agreements, and regulatory compliance.

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering / Science / Business (Mechanical, Chemical, Production preferred).

  • 3–7 years of B2B industrial sales experience (adhesives, tapes, specialty chemicals, industrial consumables).

  • Demonstrated success in solution selling and margin-focused negotiations.

  • Strong customer-facing and presentation skills.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with Automotive, Electronics, Packaging, Construction, or General Manufacturing OEMs.

  • Exposure to process lines such as lamination, bonding, gasketing, assembly, surface preparation.

  • Proficiency in CRM systems (Salesforce preferred), Excel, and demand forecasting tools.

Core Competencies

  • Application & Product Knowledge

  • Consultative & Value-Based Selling

  • Territory Planning & Discipline

  • Negotiation & Margin Protection

  • Data-Driven Decision Making

  • Ownership & Accountability

  • Cross-Functional Collaboration

Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)

  • Revenue vs OP; OLCG contribution

  • NBD wins (number/value) & conversion cycle time

  • Funnel coverage (≥3×) & forecast accuracy

  • Visit productivity & trial-to-commercialization rate

  • DSO adherence & collection efficiency

  • Ad Merch program ROI

  • Customer retention & wallet share expansion

Travel & Work Conditions

  • 50–70% field travel within assigned territory.

  • Customer plant visits requiring PPE and adherence to safety protocols.

  • Flexibility for early/late visits aligned to production schedules.

Job Description:

Job Summary:

The Strategic Planning Manager plays a critical role in shaping 3M India’s long-term direction by driving market intelligence, strategic planning, and cross-functional alignment. This role partners closely with business and functional leaders to translate insights into actionable strategies, define measurable goals, and ensure seamless execution across the organization. By integrating resource planning, risk management, and competitive analysis, the role enables informed decision-making and positions 3M for sustainable growth and market leadership.

Key Responsibilities

Research & Industry Intelligence

  • Conduct comprehensive market research, analyze industry trends, and identify opportunities, risks, and emerging threats.

  • Continuously monitor competition—revenue, profitability, market share, product portfolio, new launches, key talent, and facilities (manufacturing/R&D).

  • Provide insights that drive informed decision-making for leadership.

Strategy Development & Annual Planning

  • Partner with CBLs, Function Leaders, Division Leaders, and Group Marketing Leaders to co-develop strategic plans aligned with 3M’s vision and long-term priorities.

  • Lead and support the annual Business-Level Strategic Planning process—review prior-year plans, recalibrate assumptions, and redesign strategies in the current business context.

  • Manage the local planning timeline (March–April) to meet global strategic planning calendars.

Goal Setting & Performance Tracking

  • Define short-term and long-term goals and translate them into measurable KPIs and success metrics.

  • Build dashboards and performance tracking mechanisms and present progress to the India Leadership Team.

Cross-functional Alignment & Integration

  • Work closely with cross-functional teams to ensure strategic plans are integrated across the organization.

  • Understand resource and CAPEX requirements to enable seamless execution of strategic priorities.

Resource & Budget Planning

  • Collaborate with functional leaders to assess resource needs and oversee onboarding activities aligned with the strategic plan.

  • Recommend allocation of resources and budgets to support prioritized initiatives.

Risk Identification & Mitigation

  • Identify key business risks and develop mitigation strategies to ensure successful plan execution.

Communication & Stakeholder Engagement

  • Prepare and deliver strategic updates, reviews, and recommendations to area and global executives, internal teams, and external partners.

  • Create executive-level presentations that clearly articulate insights and strategic direction.

External Representation & Ecosystem Engagement

  • Represent 3M at industry forums and external events.

  • Build strong external networks with industry leaders, regulatory bodies, and key stakeholders to identify collaboration opportunities and stay ahead of market shifts.

Qualifications

  • Education: Master’s degree in Business Administration, Economics, Strategy, or related field.

  • Experience: Prior experience in strategic planning, business analysis, management consulting, or related strategic roles.

  • Skills:

    • Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities

    • Excellent communication, storytelling, and presentation skills

    • Proficiency in strategy frameworks, market analysis tools, and planning software

    • Ability to translate insights into actionable strategic recommendations

Personal Attributes

  • Visionary & Strategic Thinking: Ability to anticipate future trends and translate them into strategic direction.

  • Detail Orientation: Strong focus on accuracy, structure, and managing complex projects.

  • Leadership & Influence: Proven ability to lead cross-functional workstreams and influence stakeholders at all levels.

Learn more about 3M’s creative solutions to the world’s problems at www.3M.com or on Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn @3M.

At 3M, we apply science in collaborative ways to improve everyday life. Role Overview

The Regional Sales Manager (RSM) – CBG will lead the sales, distribution, and retail execution strategy for the assigned region. This role is responsible for driving business growth through effective distributor management, channel expansion, and frontline team leadership. The position demands a balance of strategic planning, operational excellence, and people management to deliver sustainable and profitable growth in alignment with 3M’s values and business priorities.

Sales & Distribution Excellence

Team Leadership

Channel & Distributor Management

  • Appoint and manage distributors/super stockists ensuring right investment, infrastructure, and service levels.

  • Monitor ROI, claims, and ensure timely financial settlements and adherence to 3M policies.

  • Strengthen trade partnerships through consistent engagement and transparent communication.

Business & Market Insights

  • Analyze market trends, channel dynamics, and competitor activities to identify growth opportunities.

  • Provide regular inputs to marketing and category teams on pricing, promotions, and product performance.

Operational Governance & Reporting

  • Ensure accurate and timely reporting on sales performance, forecasts, budgets, and trade spends.

  • Manage regional budgets effectively and ensure adherence to compliance, ethical, and operational standards.

Key Accountabilities

  • Achieving sales revenue and profitability targets for the region.

  • Driving execution excellence in distribution, visibility, and trade programs.

  • Timely and accurate claim settlements and financial control.

  • Building strong customer relationships and ensuring high satisfaction levels.

  • Adherence to 3M’s business ethics, compliance, and operational governance.

Qualifications & Experience

  • MBA or equivalent postgraduate qualification.

  • 10–15 years of relevant experience in sales, distribution, and channel management—preferably from FMCG, Consumer Durables, or allied industries.

  • Strong business acumen with proven success in leading regional teams and managing large-scale distributor networks.

  • Excellent communication, analytical, and stakeholder management skills.

  • Prior experience or familiarity with the assigned region is preferred.

Job Description:

Job Description

Advanced Application Development Engineer – Application Development & Automation

Collaborating with Innovative 3Mers

Choosing where to start and grow your career has a major impact on your professional and personal life, so it’s equally important you know that the company that you choose to work at, and its leaders, will support and guide you. With a wide variety of people, global locations, technologies and products, 3M is a place where you can collaborate with other curious, creative 3Mers.

The Impact You’ll Make in this Role
As a Application Development Engineer – Application & Automation in 3M’s Automotive & Aerospace Solutions Laboratory, you will have the opportunity to tap into your curiosity and collaborate with some of the most innovative people around the world. The successful candidate will have a strong mechanical aptitude and excel in collaborating with others in the laboratory to revolutionize standard manual processes and invent new, automated solutions for our industry.

Here, you will make an impact by:

  • Playing a key role in customer interaction & specifying 3M Automotive Automated solutions at OEM’s & Tiers
  • Leading the way on application development including responsibilities for ideation, documentation, feasibility analysis, and validation
  • Plays crucial role in defining standard processes and tools to accelerate implementation of physical automation solutions
  • Collaborating with and guide multiple teams to ensure the success of application implementation, including marketing, external industry partners, and customers
  • Providing application support and training to customers, including in-person visits
  • Gaining a complete understanding of the application effectiveness by leading field trials and internal testing along with analyzing and presenting findings
  • Striving to remain ahead of pending changes to industry standards, regulations, and best practices.
  • Developing and testing automation solutions leveraging technologies such as robotics, computer vision, machine learning and other industrial automation and software technologies.
  • Facilitating complex problem-solving, collaborating across functional groups, and leading development efforts for new customer solutions.

Your Skills and Expertise 

To set you up for success in this role from day one, 3M requires (at a minimum) the following qualifications:

  • Bachelor or Master’s degree in MECHATRONICS including FBD, Ladder Logic Sequential Function chart, Perl Shells Scripting. (completed and verified prior to start)
  • 3-5 years of experience providing technical support to customers in technical and/or application engineering role in a private, public, government, or military environment
  • Data driven / tech savvy.
  • Proven experience in presenting himself/company in external conferences/Events
  • Demonstrate lab to field /scale up experience for multiple products in his tenure
  • To provide Design Engineering in the development of various programs
  • To support prototyping and sampling till scale-up of new programs
  • To provide in-plant application training support to Customers
  • To obtain prototype approvals and tooled up Sample approvals during Program Development from Customers
  • Train sales & marketing team on various products and their applications
  • To evaluate and audit performance of products as per Customer Specific Requirements
  • To provide, generate and maintain TS16949 / QMS documentation
  • To provide a timely and optimally elaborate response and corrective action plan to Customer Complaints
  • To continuously pursue 3M Six-Sigma methodology in various work practices
  • Bachelor of Science degree in industrial engineering, systems engineering, mechanical engineering, electrical engineering, or other engineering field (completed and verified prior to start)

Learn more about 3M’s creative solutions to the world’s problems at www.3M.com or on Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn @3M.

Strong analytical, data-driven, and tech-savvy mindset. Excellent communication skills with fluency in English and comfort working in a global environment. Self-motivated, collaborative, and capable of working independently. Preferred Qualifications: Experience in customer-facing technical roles in OEM/Tier supplier environments. Job Description:

Job Title: Associate Specialist-FP&A

Collaborate with Innovative 3Mers Around the World:

Choosing where to start and grow your career has a major impact on your professional and personal life, so it’s equally important you know that the company that you choose to work at, and its leaders, will support and guide you. With a wide variety of people, global locations, technologies and products, 3M is a place where you can collaborate with other curious, creative 3Mers.

The Impact You’ll Make in this Role:

 We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Finance Analyst to join our Finance team, Bangalore. In this role, you will work cross functionally to deliver insightful, actionable analytics and influential reporting packages to drive change and improved financial performance.

  • Develop, consolidate and present insightful and robust qualitative and quantitative financial analysis. Produce weekly / Monthly / Quarterly and ad hoc reporting of financial results and drivers in FP&A – Orders, Sales, Gross Margin & OPEX; Build Forecasts & participate in Budgets
  • Collaborate with finance leaders to develop a standard catalogue of financial reports and key operational metrics.
  • Prepare daily/weekly/monthly Sales reports highlighting key drivers, risks & opportunities, currency impact, global macros, and red target opportunities. Tracking Organic Sales Growth, Gross Margin, EBITDA conversion, etc.- GAAP and non-GAAP reporting
  • Monitor and analyse the daily Orders data; Prepare “Daily Order Forecast” report, track orders KPIs like Backlog, Takt time, etc.
  • Periodic reporting of Gross-to-Net Sales, and analysing the associated drivers of Cash discounts, rebate, market development fund, etc.
  • Track & report OPEX – R&D, SG&A cost; prepare variance analysis, Forecasting and Budging
  • Prepare periodic inventory reports, KPIs, dashboards, with focus on inventory forecasting, seasonality trends, Working/Non-working inventory, etc.
  • Conduct in-depth analysis of AR, DSO, DIO, and Cash flow data, and to assist business in developing related month-end/quarter-end reports.
  • Drive value-add change by assisting in simplifying processes, accelerating automation / digitization and enhancing analytics function
  • Partner across Corporate and Business Groups FP&A and regional teams to perform ad hoc requests

Your Skills and Expertise:

  • Experience: Minimum 2-4 Years of relevant FP&A work experience
  • Education:  CA/CMA /MBA (Finance)
  • Analytical Skills: Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.
  • Technical Skills: Proficiency in Power BI, Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint, familiarity with financial modelling and data analysis tools.
  • Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
  • Team Player: Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment and manage multiple tasks simultaneously.
  • Initiative: Self-motivated with a proactive approach to learning and contributing to the team.

Supporting Your Well-being

3M offers many programs to help you live your best life – both physically and financially. To ensure competitive pay and benefits, 3M regularly benchmarks with other companies that are comparable in size and scope.

Learn more about 3M’s creative solutions to the world’s problems at www.3M.com or on Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn @3M.

Job Description:

Business Development & Portfolio Leader – Infrastructure Coating Business

Collaborate with Innovative 3Mers Around the World

Choosing where to grow your career has a major impact on your professional and personal life, so it’s equally important you know that the company that you choose to work at, and its leaders, will support and guide you. With a wide variety of people, global locations, technologies and products, 3M is a place where you can collaborate with other curious, creative 3Mers.

This position provides an opportunity to transition from other private, public, government or military experience to a 3M career.

The Impact You’ll Make in this Role
 

As the Business Development & Portfolio Leader for 3M’s Infrastructure Coating Business, you will have the opportunity to tap into your curiosity and collaborate with some of the most innovative people around the world. Here, you will make an impact by:

  • Identify, develop, and maintain sales opportunities for assigned countries

  • Ensure global, and region-specific, Infrastructure Coatings profit center sales and margin objectives are met or exceeded

  • Obtain customer and internal input to develop portfolio strategic plan, aligned with divisional strategic plan. Execution of this plan will be an ongoing partnership with local and global business leaders, in order to accomplish common goals

  • Work directly with division executives and senior 3M International subsidiary management allocate appropriate resources

  • Provide leadership, coaching and direction to business Area and subsidiary personnel

  • Undertake portfolio management and pricing activities to enable gross-margin and plant utilization objectives

Key Activities will include :

  • Strategic business growth: Develop and implement commercial and sales strategies to expand the company's presence in the pipeline coating market. This includes identifying and pursuing new business opportunities with existing and potential customers.

  • Sales and pipeline management: Build and maintain a robust sales pipeline by generating new leads and tracking progress. You are accountable for achieving annual sales targets and managing the profit and loss (P&L) for the business line.

  • Client and account management: Nurture and expand relationships with key clients, which may include major oil and gas, industrial, or energy companies. Conduct contract negotiations and ensure customer requirements are met.

  • Market analysis and intelligence: Monitor market trends, customer needs, and competitor activities to inform business strategy and identify opportunities for innovation.

  • Product and service development: Collaborate with research and development (R&D) and marketing teams to launch new products and services that align with market demands.

  • Performance reporting: Prepare and present regular reports to senior management on sales performance, market trends, budget achievements, and forecast accuracy.

  • Pricing: Understand the Epoxy Raw Material costing, manufacturing cost structure, and external competitive environment, to provide list price and project specific contract price guidance to the sales and commercial center organisations globally.

  • Technical collaboration: Work with engineers and technical support staff to provide customers with tailored solutions and ensure a deep understanding of product advantages and application techniques.

Your Skills and Expertise 
 

To set you up for success in this role from day one, 3M requires (at a minimum) the following qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree or higher (completed and verified prior to start) from an accredited institution

  • Ten (10) years of experience in a business or sales function, with a proven track record of driving successful growth results in a private, public, government or military environment

Additional qualifications that could help you succeed even further in this role include:

  • Bachelor's degree, or higher, in Chemical Engineering

  • Experience in the Performance Coatings or Oil & Gas industry

  • Experience at the global or regional level, with a deep understanding of multiple local markets and cultural dynamics

  • Strong analytical skills and in-depth knowledge of sales management, including planning and execution, key account development, negotiation of trading terms, new market development, and team coaching

  • Strong operational and problem-solving skills

  • Proven leadership abilities, with strong negotiation and influencing skills

  • Proven ability to lead and inspire cross-functional teams to achieve business objectives

Work location:

  • This role follows an on-site working model, requiring the employee to work at least four days a week at a locally applicable 3M office.

  • Jakarta, Indonesia

  • Bagalore, India

​

Travel: May include up to 40% travel

Relocation: May be authorized

 

Must be legally authorized to work in country of employment without sponsorship for employment visa status (e.g., H1B status).

 

 Supporting Your Well-being

3M offers many programs to help you live your best life – both physically and financially. To ensure competitive pay and benefits, 3M regularly benchmarks with other companies that are comparable in size and scope.

The Product Developer– Abrasives Systems will be responsible for the development, engineering, and localization of nonwoven, coated, and portable bonded abrasive products. This role requires strong hands‑on technical capability, deep materials and process knowledge, and the ability to translate innovation into robust, manufacturable products through phase‑gate commercialization.

The individual will collaborate closely with global, area and India R&D, manufacturing, quality, supply chain, and marketing teams to deliver differentiated abrasive solutions aligned with customer needs and regional market requirements.

Key Responsibilities

Product Development

  • Develop and engineer nonwoven, coated, and portable bonded abrasive products from concept through commercialization.
  • Apply materials science, chemistry, and process engineering principles to optimize performance, cost, quality, and manufacturability.
  • Lead formulation development, process optimization, and product robustness activities.
  • Build and validate proof‑of‑concepts, prototypes, and pilot‑scale trials.
  • Engineer products for desired cut, finish, flexibility, conformability, and durability.
  • Work hands‑on with wet chemistry, coating, curing, and web handling equipment.
  • Address performance attributes such as cut rate, life, heat management, and surface finish.
  • Develop resin‑bonded cut‑off wheels and grinding wheels.
  • Optimize wheel properties including strength, safety, cut performance, and operator comfort.
  • Support molding, pressing, curing, and finishing processes.

Localization & Manufacturing Support

  • Localize global technologies to regional raw materials, suppliers, and manufacturing capabilities.
  • Qualify alternate raw materials and conduct equivalency and benchmarking studies.
  • Provide technical support during scale‑up, technology transfer, and plant start‑ups.
  • Troubleshoot quality, yield, and performance issues using root‑cause analysis and data‑driven methods.

Required Qualifications

  • Master’s or PhD in Materials Science, Chemical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Polymer Science, or related technical discipline.
  • 7–10 years of experience in R&D, product development, or product engineering within abrasives or related materials industries.
  • Demonstrated experience in nonwoven, coated, and/or portable bonded abrasives product development.
  • Strong technical expertise in thermoset resin systems (phenolic, epoxy, urethane).
  • Hands‑on experience with wet chemistry, formulation development, and pilot/manufacturing equipment.
  • Experience with stage‑gate commercialization processes.
  • Proven ability to support manufacturing scale‑up and quality consistency.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience generating patents or IP disclosures.
  • Knowledge of abrasive product safety standards and testing methods.
  • Experience localizing products for cost‑sensitive or emerging markets.
  • Demonstrated leadership in cross‑functional or global project teams.
  • Familiarity with continuous improvement, lean, or Six Sigma methodologies.

Key Competencies

  • Strong problem‑solving and analytical skills
  • Hands‑on, experimental mindset
  • Customer‑focused product engineering approach
  • Ability to influence without authority
  • Passion for innovation and learning

Learn more about 3M’s creative solutions to the world’s problems at www.3M.com or on Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn @3M.

Job Description:

SUMMARY

The Product Development Engineer supports the design, formulation, and commercialization of advanced materials used in electronic components and their assemblies, such as adhesives, tapes, conductive materials, thermal materials, and assembly materials. The role focuses on developing innovative solutions that meet customer performance requirements and align with business growth objectives.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Develop new materials or enhance existing products for electronic applications such as bonding, shielding, grounding, semiconductors, displays, interconnects, or thermal management.
  • Design and execute laboratory experiments, analyze data, and document results.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams including manufacturing, quality, marketing, and application engineering.
  • Support scale-up, pilot production runs, and technology transfer to manufacturing.
  • Partner with customers, applications engineering and commercial teams to understand application needs and translate them into product specifications.
  • Support testing and qualification projects of existing products.
  • Evaluate competitive products and emerging technologies to guide development direction.
  • Ensure product designs comply with relevant safety, regulatory, and reliability requirements.
  • Prepare technical reports, presentations, and documentation supporting project decisions and product launches.
  • Contribute with customer insights, new product ideas and intellectual property.
  • Conform with company policies, including Code of Conduct and Compliance.

THE IMPACT YOU’LL MAKE IN THIS ROLE

As a Product Development Engineer, you will have the opportunity to tap into your curiosity and collaborate with some of the most innovative and diverse people around the world. Here, you will make an impact by:

  • Working with global and local Applications Engineering, Product Development, Commercial and Marketing teams to develop advanced materials that enable next-generation electronic devices.
  • Accelerating business growth by advancing products from concept to commercialization efficiently.
  • Enhancing manufacturing readiness and product quality through robust scale‑up and validation.
  • Represent 3M portfolio for Electronics to our customers. through presentations, training, conferences and other technical interactions.
  • Providing launch support for new product programs by generating supporting documentation, demonstration tools, videos, and training materials.
  • Building a deep understanding of customer needs and competition for the electronics market and provide “Voice of Customer,” new product ideas and competitive analysis.
  • Develop lab infrastructure to support business plans, plan for equipment, inputs for lab capex planning, work with engineering for procurement of equipment by providing right specifications.

QUALIFICATIONS:

  • Master’s degree or higher from any reputed Institution/ University with polymer science, chemistry, materials science, or a related field as the major subject.
  • Experience with polymer systems, adhesives, coatings, films, or electronic materials is preferred.
  • Minimum of 3-5 years of work experience in R&D and product development. Preferably, in a related role developing materials for electronics assembly.
  • Required experience handling various types of samples for performing tests such as tensile, mechanical shock, vibration, thermos-mechanical, electrical insulation, contact resistance tests, etc.
  • Required experience in testing mechanical, chemical and electrical properties of materials. Proficiency in conducting product evaluations, performance testing, failure-mode analysis, and troubleshooting.
  • Proficient in designing experiments, testing methods, and standard operating procedures.
  • Comfortable in handling testing equipment, and analytical instruments.
  • Good understanding about IP protocols and procedures.
  • Should be a team player, able to plan and work independently with minimum supervision.
  • Strong problem-solving, experimental design, and data-analysis skills.
  • Ability to work effectively in cross-functional, project-based environments.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • This role may require about 10% of domestic travel.

Job Description:

The Impact You’ll Make in this Role

Sr. Legal Analyst – will be part of the Global Legal Service Center (GLSC) and perform a variety of data and information management activities under the supervision of 3M Legal Affairs attorneys, managers, and/or supervisors. This position will report to the GLSC Multiple Pillar Work Group Lead and work closely with 3M Legal Affairs paralegals. The Sr. Legal Analyst is an experienced eDiscovery professional with foundational knowledge of the Electronic Discovery Reference Model and the collection of electronically stored data for preservation and review.   

Responsibilities:

Under the direction of 3M Legal Affairs attorneys and paralegals, the Sr. Legal Analyst will perform tasks related to gathering, organizing, administering, and/or managing information and databases for litigation, investigations, or early case assessment. These responsibilities may include:

  • Data Management – Collects and analyzes data for litigation preservation and review and interfaces with outside review vendors as needed. Assists in the preparation of project plans to manage data that may include cost estimates, timelines, and resources to meet project deliverables. Organizes data and performs analysis to ensure legal requirements are met. Develops knowledge of policies, regulations, and trends related to information management in the legal environment. Initiates requests, inquiries, and follow-up with clients and/or vendors. Performs basic analysis to identify efficient information management methods and tools. Designs and provides reports and summaries based on clients’ needs.
  • Database Management. Serves on teams that evaluate database tools and makes recommendations for system design and enhancements. Responsible for database integrity by reviewing data and resolving discrepancies. Recommends process improvements and technology options for managing information. Troubleshoots, evaluates, tests, and assists in implementation of upgrades and updates to database(s). Provides documentation for user and training manuals.
  • Collaboration and Communication. Establishes and maintains relationships with attorneys, employees, outside counsel, and vendors. Responds to routine inquiries and requests from clients. May participate in special projects.

Your Skills and Expertise

To set you up for success in this role from day one, 3M is looking for candidates who must have the following qualifications:

  • Law degree preferred; candidates with other degrees with experience in Microsoft Purview may also apply.
  • Minimum of 5-7 years of experience as legal analyst, preferably in similar MNCs, Law firms, or Global Capability Centers.
  • Proficiency with Microsoft Purview and/or Nuix Discover or any other eDiscovery tools
  • Familiarity with US litigation processes and related documentation.
  • Knowledge of databases and corporate systems relevant to litigation.
  • Strong organizational skills: ability to establish priorities, balance multiple tasks and deadlines at one time and effectively manage competing priorities.
  • Effective interpersonal skills and a collaborative team player, including the ability to communicate in written and spoke English.

Additional qualifications that could help you succeed even further in this role include

  • Any additional eDiscovery or data management course certification
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office, especially Microsoft Excel
  • Familiarity with AI / Gen AI tools as they relate to eDiscovery

Relocation Assistance:

  • In country relocation assistance is not authorized for this role.
  • Must be legally authorized to work in country of employment without sponsorship for employment visa status (e.g., H1B status).

Here, you can apply your talent in bold ways that matter.

Job Description:

Background & Market Context

The electronics sector in India continues to be one of the fastest growing industries, supported by strong policy initiatives, manufacturing incentives, and increasing localization of global supply chains. Key segments such as Mobile Phones, IT Hardware, Consumer Electronics, Automotive Electronics, Wearables, LED Lighting, EV components, and PCBA manufacturing are expanding rapidly.

South India & West India have emerged as a major electronics manufacturing hub — spanning Chennai, Bangalore, Hosur, Coimbatore, Hyderabad, Pune, Ahmedabad, & Mumbai. Within 3M's Transportation & Electronics Business Group, the Display & Electronics Product Platform requires focused, senior individual contributor leadership in this region to accelerate growth, strengthen distribution and converter networks, and deepen engagement with OEM and CM customers.

Role Summary

The Regional Sales & Channel Specialist – South & West Zone is a senior individual contributor role responsible for driving sales growth, expanding the distributor and converter ecosystem, and directly managing strategic key accounts across South India. The role operates with a high degree of ownership and autonomy, requiring strong commercial instincts, technical fluency in tape conversion, and deep capability in channel development and key account management.

This is a hands-on execution role — designed to unlock the full commercial potential of a high-priority, under-penetrated regional market.

Key Responsibilities

A. South & West Zone Territory Execution & Commercial Ownership

  • Own and drive structured sales execution across the South & West India territory, covering major electronics clusters in Chennai, Bangalore, Hosur, Coimbatore, Hyderabad, Pune, Ahmedabad and other key cities in the zones.
  • Develop and execute territory plans with clear account prioritization, pipeline targets, and coverage strategies.
  • Maintain rigorous visit cadence with customers and channel partners to ensure active opportunity progression.
  • Act as the senior commercial point of contact for the South & West Zone, providing coverage depth and market presence currently absent in the region.

B. Key Account Management

  • Directly manage and develop major OEM, CM, ODM, and Automotive Electronics accounts
  • Drive design-in programs, qualification cycles, and accelerate decision timelines at key accounts.
  • Build and sustain strong executive-level relationships to deepen 3M's strategic positioning.
  • Identify and pursue adjacency opportunities within existing key accounts to expand share of wallet.

C. Distributor & Converter Network Expansion

  • Define and execute a structured plan to expand distribution and converter presence across South & West India — a critical capability gap that the role is specifically designed to address.
  • Evaluate existing 3M converters and identify the right partners aligned to electronics manufacturing opportunities.
  • Design and implement distributor sales representative programs to improve reach and in-market execution.
  • Leverage 3M IATD converter networks to strengthen regional capability and technical support.

D. Tape Conversion & Technical Fluency

  • Apply strong working knowledge of tape conversion processes — including slitting, die-cutting, lamination, and value-add operations — to advise customers and converters.
  • Optimize material flow from source plant through converter to end customer, reducing friction and improving cycle times.
  • Act as a technical commercial resource for customers evaluating material solutions.

E. SFDC Governance & Pipeline Management

  • Maintain high-quality SFDC hygiene including opportunity progression, forecasting accuracy, and activity logging.
  • Conduct structured pipeline reviews and take ownership of improving close-won ratios in the South & West Zone.
  • Provide accurate and timely sales reporting to the India sales leadership team.

F. Sampling Center & Local Commercialization

  • Drive utilization of the sampling center for customer demos, product validation, and proof-of-concept programs.
  • Lead commercialization of local slitting SKUs by identifying market opportunities and coordinating with marketing, lab, and supply chain teams.
  • Accelerate time-to-market for new material solutions by managing cross-functional commercialization projects independently.

G. Market Intelligence & Opportunity Development

  • Track new manufacturing investments, program announcements, and competitor movements across South & West India.
  • Build proactive account plans to capture emerging opportunities ahead of the competition.
  • Provide structured market feedback to the broader India team to inform product and go-to-market strategy.

Skills, Expertise & Qualifications

Minimum Requirements:

  • Bachelor's degree in Electronics or Mechanical Engineering.
  • 8–12 years of sales experience in Electronics, Automotive Electronics, OEM/CM/ODM, or Converter ecosystems.
  • Proven track record as a high-performing individual contributor managing complex accounts and channel programs.
  • Strong understanding of electronics manufacturing processes and tape conversion (slitting, die-cutting, lamination).
  • Experience independently designing and executing distributor and channel development programs.
  • Working knowledge of SFDC and structured sales methodologies.

Behavioral Competencies:

  • High ownership mindset — takes initiative and drives outcomes without needing a supervisory structure to operate effectively.
  • Strong commercial and technical credibility with OEM, CM, and Converter partners.
  • Structured, data-driven, and commercially focused in planning and execution.
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills — able to engage across customer levels and internal functions.
  • Genuine passion for channel development, ecosystem building, and regional market expansion.
  • Self-directed and comfortable operating with autonomy in a geographically dispersed role.

Learn more about 3M’s creative solutions to the world’s problems at www.3M.com or on Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn @3M.

Safety is a core value at 3M. All employees are expected to contribute to a strong Environmental Health and Safety (EHS) culture by following safety policies, identifying hazards, and engaging in continuous improvement.
